title: White Worms in Dog's Eye: Triage Guide and Next Steps
meta_desc: If you see white worms in your dog's eye, it is a high-priority veterinary issue. Learn about Thelazia, treatment urgency, and how to help your vet.
content_html:
What is it?

If you notice thin, white, thread-like organisms moving across the surface of your dog's eye or tucked beneath the eyelids, your pet likely has a parasitic infection called Thelazia, commonly known as "eye worms." These parasites are typically transmitted by certain types of flies and live in the tear ducts and conjunctival sac of the eye.

Is it an Emergency?

The urgency level for this condition is High. While it may not be immediately life-threatening, it is a source of intense physical discomfort and pain for your dog. If left untreated, the movement of the worms can cause corneal ulcers, chronic inflammation, and even permanent blindness. You should contact your veterinarian or an emergency clinic within 24 hours to arrange for professional removal and a prescription for appropriate antiparasitic medication.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

Taking a clear photo or short video of the worms in the eye can be a vital tool for your veterinary team. Because the worms often hide behind the third eyelid or move quickly when the eye is disturbed, a photo provides proof of the infestation. This allows the clinic to prioritize your case and prepare the necessary numbing agents and specialized instruments needed for safe removal before you even walk through the door.

faqs.question: Can I remove the worm at home with tweezers?
faqs.answer: No. You should never attempt to remove a worm from your dog's eye yourself. Any sudden movement from your dog could result in a puncture wound to the eye, and professional sedation or local anesthesia is often required to safely remove the parasites without causing further trauma.

faqs.question: Can humans get eye worms from their dogs?
faqs.answer: While rare, some species of Thelazia are zoonotic, meaning they can be transmitted to humans through the same fly vectors that infect dogs. It is important to treat your pet immediately and practice good hand hygiene.

faqs.question: What are the symptoms of eye worms besides seeing the worms?
faqs.answer: Common signs include excessive tearing (epiphora), squinting, redness of the whites of the eyes, conjunctivitis, and your dog pawing at their face due to irritation.
